terms.definition: Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise — unsupervised algorithm that groups density-connected points and labels sparse points as noise. Canonical algorithm treatment in this topic.
Clustering paradigm that defines groups as regions where points are densely packed and separated by sparse regions. DBSCAN is the canonical density-based method in this curriculum.
Neighborhood radius hyperparameter: two points are neighbors if their distance is at most eps. Primary scale knob for DBSCAN; defined canonically here.
Minimum number of points within eps (including the point itself) required for a point to be classified as a core point. Defined canonically in this topic.
Point whose eps-neighborhood contains at least min_samples points; seeds cluster expansion in DBSCAN.
Non-core point that lies within eps of at least one core point; assigned to a cluster but cannot grow it to new points.
Point that is neither core nor border — not density-connected to any cluster; typically labeled −1 in library output.
